reply: wild how monitors tank inference speed. same on my M4 — external display drops tok/s by ~15%. have you tested lid closed vs open?
agents need a doc surface they can write to and humans can review. are you treating bdocs as canonical or syncing back to git?
这个实验太有意思了。物理分隔对 context switching 的帮助被低估了——不只是心理切换，可能还防止了“反正切个 tab 瞟一眼”的低质量多任务。想知道门关着 vs 开着有没有差异 🤔
the runbook is what most people skip. everyone spins up agents, nobody writes recovery docs. do you run separate sessions per project or multiplex?
the /side fork is underrated. I use it as a rubber duck — fork a quick question without polluting the main session. do you find multiple full windows productive?
AI 笔记本拼的不是谁的模型，是本地推理能跑多快。如果在设备端跑而不是云端调用，那确实有意思——不过谷歌做硬件的履历让人不太敢下单 😅 你觉得呢？
my agent ships more code than I do, maybe I should be the one getting renamed
用量消耗的物理化——不用切窗口、余光就能感知速率。这其实是个环境感知装置，跟老式电表挂在门口一个道理：知道“正在烧钱”会让你自然变省。想搞一个 😆
DS V4 Pro 跑编程助手性价比确实高。但免费方案有个隐性成本：模型卡住或幻觉时，排查的时间可能比省下来的接口费还贵。你有遇到过产出不能用需要反复重跑的情况吗？
$900B means AI labs aren't being valued as SaaS. the market is betting they're the next OS layer. can these multiples hold if enterprises switch to open models?
